Steps: Send POST request to /api/authenticate with valid username/email and password
Expected: Response contains a valid authentication token

Steps: Send POST request to /api/authenticate with invalid username/email or password
Expected: Response contains unauthorized status and error message without sensitive information

TestName: Token Validation Success with Valid Token
Preconditions: Obtain a valid and non-expired JWT token via authentication
Steps: Send GET request to /api/protected-resource with valid JWT token in Authorization header
Expected: Request is authorized and proceeds to return requested resource

TestName: Token Validation Failure with Expired Token
Steps: Send GET request to /api/protected-resource with expired JWT token in Authorization header
Expected: Request rejected with unauthorized response indicating expired token

TestName: Token Validation Failure with Tampered Token
Preconditions: Create a tampered JWT token with invalid signature
Steps: Send GET request to /api/protected-resource with tampered JWT token in Authorization header
Expected: Request rejected with unauthorized response indicating invalid token

TestName: Request Rejected When Authentication Token Missing
Steps: Send GET request to /api/protected-resource without Authorization header
Expected: Request rejected with unauthorized response indicating missing credentials

TestName: Authentication Failure Does Not Leak Sensitive Information
Steps: Send POST request to /api/authenticate with invalid credentials
Expected: Response includes generic error message without revealing sensitive security details

Steps: Send GET request to /api/protected-resource with malformed JWT token in Authorization header
Expected: Request rejected with unauthorized response due to invalid token structure

TestName: Verify Authenticated User Identity Context on Authorized Request
Preconditions: Authenticate and obtain valid JWT token for user
Steps: Send POST request to /api/user-actions with valid JWT token in Authorization header including user-specific action data
Expected: System processes action associating it with authenticated user identity

TestName: Consistent Status Code for Authentication Failure
Steps: Send POST request to /api/authenticate with invalid credentials
Send POST request to another authentication-related endpoint with invalid credentials (if applicable)
Expected: Both responses return consistent status code (e.g., 401) without contradictory behaviors

TestName: Reject Expired Token for Protected Resource Access
Preconditions: Expire the JWT token prior to request
Steps: Send GET request to /api/financial-data with expired JWT token in Authorization header
Expected: Request rejected with an unauthorized response indicating expired token
